summaries.101275: The root cause is that Android TV and IR remote devices send the Select key mapped to the physical key `Control Right` without setting modifier flags, violating Flutter's assertion in `raw_keyboard.dart` that requires keysPressed to be non-empty before a key-down event. The issue is confirmed on Android TV devices (TX 3 mini, Linux devices with custom IR remotes), Windows with USB barcode scanners (Shift Left key triggers same assert), and Android on Linux. A workaround via a linked comment on issue #65233 involves using the `onKey` method on FocusNode instead. Currently stalled with no fix landed.
summaries.120724: The core technical finding from comments is that on iOS, dictation sends individual deltas sequentially, but each delta assumes the previous delta's result without waiting for app-side modifications, making it impossible for apps like super_editor to inject corrections between deltas. Renzo-Olivares investigated iOS dictation APIs and found no reliable way to detect dictation start/end on iOS 16+. Engine PR #43267 (flutter/engine) was identified as providing a positive improvement, with one user confirming significantly better behavior after it landed in beta, though extra spaces were still being added. The issue remains partially open with the assignee removed due to inactivity.
summaries.95958: Comments confirm the bug affects iOS Safari and Chrome on Android (web platform) on stable and master channels through at least Flutter 3.9, with the cursor failing to move to tapped positions in multiline TextFields. The root cause is identified as Flutter's inability to sync scroll state between the underlying hidden input element and the visible content, combined with inability to hide the native selection overlay on iOS (see #99918). Issue #120613 was linked as a proposed solution to address the underlying architecture. A native HTML element workaround was suggested. Currently stalled with no fix; still marked P1 but with no active assignee.
summaries.139230: Comments identify the root cause as a lack of synchronization when the TLHC platform view is resized: the old image reader is torn down and a null image inserted before the new reader is populated, causing frames to drop during the gap. Jonahwilliams provided detailed analysis of the three-thread sequence (UI, Platform, Raster). Engine PR #49201 was attempted and eliminated dropped frames but introduced image stretching as a new artifact. The user reported that Hybrid Composition (HC) meanwhile developed new regressions with janky screen animations and screen-wide flickering when HC visibility changes. Currently stalled with assignee removed; users remain on HC as an imperfect workaround.
summaries.85514: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on both stable (2.2.2) and master channels on Linux. The cause is that when `onKey` changes focus via `nextFocus()`, the key event propagates to the newly focused node and triggers another `nextFocus()`, resulting in focus jumping two fields instead of one. No fix or PR was linked. The issue appears stalled with only triage confirmation and a cc to @gspencergoog.
summaries.45110: Comments identify the root cause: `CupertinoTabScaffold` calls `_focusActiveTab()` in `didChangeDependencies()` unconditionally, which calls `setFirstFocus` on the tab's FocusScopeNode and steals focus from the TextField in the AppBar whenever dependencies change (e.g., keyboard showing). A user-provided workaround modifies `_focusActiveTab()` to only call `setFirstFocus` when the `tabFocusNodes` list is newly initialized. The bug was reproduced on stable 3.3 and master 3.6 by a triager. A final comment asks if it still reproduces on latest master, with no resolution.
summaries.107462: Comments confirm that `TextInputClient` exhibits the same cross-platform inconsistency as `DeltaTextInputClient`. The root cause is a macOS-specific behavior where the platform sends `updateEditingValueWithDeltas` callbacks in response to `setEditingState`, while Windows does not. One commenter suggested the Windows behavior is more reasonable. No fix or PR was proposed. Issue appears stalled.
summaries.69281: Comments confirm the bug (TextField text/fill misplaced when window is resized to small sizes on Windows) is reproducible on both stable 3.3 and master 3.6. The triager was unable to reproduce the exact misplacement seen by the reporter but observed visually abnormal output at minimum window sizes. No root cause was identified, no PR was linked, and the issue remains stalled.
summaries.118547: A triager confirmed the bug is reproducible on macOS master (3.7.0-pre) using a simplified repro: wrapping a single TextField in a Tooltip causes Chinese IME input to fail when the mouse moves over autocomplete suggestions. Removing the Tooltip fixes the issue. A label correction note points out the issue was mislabeled as macOS when it primarily affects Windows. No root cause analysis or fix was proposed; currently stalled.
summaries.59541: Comments confirm the bug affects iOS and Android with external Bluetooth keyboards across many keyboard layouts (Spanish, Portuguese BR, Vietnamese, Czech, French-CA) and remains unfixed as of Flutter 3.32.7, spanning six years. A Windows fix via character combining exists at flutter/engine PR #27921 (`CharacterCombiner` class in `KeyboardManager.java`) but was never ported to iOS/Android mobile. Two community workarounds exist: a `dead_keys` pub.dev package (alpha, imperfect) and a custom `TextEditingController` with a hardcoded accented character lookup table. No official fix or PR is planned; issue is stalled and users report significant business impact.
summaries.98352: A triager confirmed the bug on Android (not iOS) where the first numeric character typed is dropped when `TextInputType.phone`, `.number`, or `.numberWithOptions()` is used with a raw `TextInputClient`. A fix was proposed in flutter/engine PR #31586 but comments from later users on Flutter 3.7.12 suggest it was never merged or the fix did not land. The assignee (@LongCatIsLooong) was removed due to inactivity; currently stalled.
summaries.140369: Comments confirm the keyboard dismiss/reappear behavior when switching between a non-obscure and obscure TextField is device-specific, reproducible on HarmonyOS 4, Vivo Android 12/13, but not on standard Android emulators or Xiaomi Android 13. The root cause on affected devices is that a security keyboard layer (preventing screen recording of passwords) grabs focus when the obscure TextField is focused. Two workarounds were posted: using `Future.delayed` to re-request focus after a delay, and calling `FocusScope.of(context).unfocus()` in an `onPointerDown` Listener wrapper. One user reports the issue resolved in Flutter >= 3.22.0, but others on physical devices report it still occurs in 3.35.3.
summaries.12920: Comments establish that implementing iOS autocorrect tooltips requires returning an accurate `CGRect` from `func firstRect(for range: UITextRange)` synchronously on the platform thread, which conflicts with Flutter's async communication model. @LongCatIsLooong investigated two approaches (blocking with CFRunLoop as done in `FlutterKeyboardManager.mm`, or async prefetching) but both have race conditions or visual glitches that remain unsolved. As of iOS 17 beta, a new regression was reported where the autocorrect highlight rectangle incorrectly highlights the most recently typed character at all times. Spellcheck (underline + context menu) has been implemented separately via engine PRs and framework PRs; the tooltip itself remains unimplemented with the assignee unassigned.
